paper_title: Multi4D: High-Fidelity Dynamic Gaussian Splatting via Multi-Level Competitive Allocation

problem_reconstruction.original_problem: Dynamic 3D Gaussian Splatting methods face a fundamental conflict between motion consistency (temporal correspondence) and visual fidelity (high-frequency rendering quality).
problem_reconstruction.reformulated_problem: How can we break the monolithic representation assumption and enable dynamic scenes to be modeled by multiple specialized sub-representations that compete to explain different aspects of the signal?
problem_reconstruction.research_formula:
Given multi-view video frames 𝒮 = {Iₜ}ₜ₌₁ᵀ, find an optimal decomposition 𝒢 = ℊₛ ∪ ℊ𝒹 ∪ ℊₜ that minimizes ℒ_total = ℒ_color + λ_sep·ℒ_sep + λ_reg·ℒ_reg + λ_div·ℒ_diversity, where ℊₛ is static, ℊ𝒹 is persistent-deformable, and ℊₜ is transient-4D, such that:
  (1) ℊ𝒹 maintains long-term temporal identity via deformation field Φ𝓰
  (2) ℊₜ captures high-frequency residuals via 4D spatiotemporal slicing
  (3) Shared transmittance in a unified renderer induces competitive allocation that suppresses redundancy
  (4) Total dynamic primitives ≪ monolithic alternatives (target: 25× fewer than 4DGS)
key_constraints: Long-term motion consistency / Gaussian identity preservation for downstream tasks
High-frequency appearance modeling (specularities, lighting shifts, fire, smoke)
Compact storage (≪ 4DGS's 2.6 GB for comparable quality)
No ground-truth motion or segmentation labels required

motion_over_factorization.problem: Deformation-based methods over-smooth high-frequency dynamics by grouping nearby motion
corresponding_modules: Competitive multi-level allocation — transient subset ℊₜ absorbs high-frequency residuals that deformation field Φ𝓰 cannot model
ℒ_diversity loss — penalizes structural similarity between ℊₜ and ℊ𝒹 outputs, forcing specialization
Self-supervised dynamic-static decomposition — prevents static content from polluting deformation modeling

temporal_over_parameterization.problem: 4D-primitive methods hallucinate millions of short-lived primitives with broken geometry
corresponding_modules: Persistent dynamic subset ℊ𝒹 — provides structured motion backbone with long-term identity
Mask-aware utility-based pruning — removes low-contribution primitives using peak visible contribution scoring
Velocity-aware lifting — provides motion prior for ℊₜ instead of random initialization
Independent per-subset densification with tight capacity control on ℊₜ

monocular_sparse_view_degradation.problem: 4D-primitive methods without holistic motion prior generalize poorly to sparse/monocular settings
corresponding_modules: Deformation field Φ𝓰 on ℊ𝒹 — provides holistic motion prior and canonical geometric constraints
Velocity-aware lifting with momentum inheritance — strong motion prior stabilizes ℊₜ under limited supervision
Spatial-temporal rescaling — prevents numerical instability from ill-conditioned covariance matrices
Dense ℊₛ from COLMAP — provides structural anchor for the scene

appearance_as_spurious_motion.problem: Deformation-based methods interpret complex appearance changes as physical motion, causing spurious warping
corresponding_modules: Transient ℊₜ with 4D slicing and 4DSH — captures time-varying appearance changes directly
ℒ_diversity — encourages ℊₜ to explain appearance residuals rather than ℊ𝒹
Geometric-only deformation field Φ𝓰 — predicts rigid motion (μₜ, rₜ) without appearance drift

semantic_feature_rendering_bottleneck.problem: Deformation-based semantic models inherit computational overhead of evaluating high-dim features per-primitive per-frame
corresponding_modules: Persistent subset ℊₚ = ℊₛ ∪ ℊ𝒹 only for semantic learning — discards transient noise
Compact dynamic Gaussians (13k vs 624k in TRASE) — enables 204 FPS feature rendering
KNN feature smoothing in canonical 3D space — enforces spatial coherence without per-frame processing
Frozen deformation field Φ𝓰 — propagates features through time without re-optimization

cross_subset_redundancy.problem: Multiple subsets could redundantly model the same regions, wasting capacity
corresponding_modules: Shared transmittance coupling — once one subset explains a region, residual-driven densification suppresses others
ℒ_diversity loss — penalizes SSIM similarity between ℊₜ and ℊ𝒹 renders
Mask-aware gating in pruning — ensures ℊₛ and ℊ𝒹 contribute only within their assigned regions
Inverse expressiveness initialization — dense COLMAP for ℊₛ, sparse for ℊ𝒹, empty for ℊₜ

architecture_diagram.input: Multi-view video: 𝒮 = {Iₜ}ₜ₌₁ᵀ with camera parameters
architecture_diagram.representation: 𝒢 = ℊₛ ∪ ℊ𝒹 ∪ ℊₜ (three Gaussian subsets)
process_flow: ℊₛ: (μ, Σ, σ, c₃D) — time invariant
ℊ𝒹: (μₜ, rₜ) = (μ, r) + Φ𝓰(μ, t) — geometric deformation via HexPlane
ℊₜ: (μ_xyz|t, Σ_xyz|t) via 4D slicing (Eq. 3); σₜ = σ·exp(−(t−μₜ)²/2Σ₄,₄)
[Phase I: 0–10k] Subset formation with all mechanisms active
Downstream: Freeze ℊₛ + ℊ𝒹, attach semantic features → 4D segmentation (Section 3.8)
outputs: Full render C_full for novel view synthesis
Persistent render Cₚ for downstream tasks (segmentation)
Transient contribution Cₜ for appearance analysis
Semantic features → 4D object masks with temporal consistency

key_equations.formula: g(x) = exp(−½ (x−μ)ᵀ Σ⁻¹ (x−μ)), Σ = R S Sᵀ Rᵀ
C(u) = Σᵢ cᵢ(d) σᵢ 𝒫ᵢ(gᵢ, u) Πⱼ₌₁ⁱ⁻¹ (1−σⱼ 𝒫ⱼ(gⱼ, u))
μ_xyz|t = μ₁:₃ + Σ₁:₃,₄ Σ₄,₄⁻¹ (t−μₜ), Σ_xyz|t = Σ₁:₃,₁:₃ − Σ₁:₃,₄ Σ₄,₄⁻¹ Σ₄,₁:₃
key_equations.purpose: 4D Gaussian slicing to 3D at timestamp t

key_equations.formula: Θₜ,ᵢ = f(gᵢ, t) per subset type
key_equations.purpose: Unified rendering projection for each subset type

key_equations.formula: wᵢ,𝐼 = max_𝐮 (σᵢ 𝒫ᵢ Π (1−σⱼ 𝒫ⱼ)) · M(u); sᵢ = β·max + (1−β)·mean
